title: Garlicky Crunchy Canadian Fish Stir-fry
description: A quick and tasty post-workout dish featuring crispy fish mixed with a vibrant garlicky sauce that's perfect for vegans looking for a filling meal.

ingredients.name: 4 oz Canadian whitefish fillets
ingredients.notes: Can be substituted with tofu or tempeh for non-meat option

ingredients.name: 2 tbsp soy sauce (or tamari for gluten-free)

instructions: Pat fish dry with paper towels.
Season fish fillets with salt and pepper on both sides. If using cashews, mix them into the seasoning for added texture.
Heat oil in a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat. Add garlic and cook until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Place seasoned fish in the pan and sear on each side for about 2-3 minutes or until golden brown and cooked through. Remove from pan and set aside.
In the same pan, add lemon juice and lime juice with paprika (if using). Stir well to combine.
Return fish to the pan and spoon sauce over evenly. Cook for an additional minute to allow flavors to meld together.
Serve immediately with chopped cashews on top.
To enhance crunchiness: You can sprinkle more cashews or sesame seeds before serving, as they also add a satisfying crunchy texture.
